name: Tuva (Russia)

colors.symbolism: courage, strength and the eternal blue sky of the Tuvan homeland
the purity of the rivers (the Bii-Khem and Kaa-Khem, sources of the Yenisei) and high moral values
Buddhist faith, wealth and prosperity of the Tuvan people

description: A light-blue field with a white pall (Y-shape) lying on its side, opening toward the hoist: two diagonal arms reach from the centreline out to the upper- and lower-hoist corners, and a single horizontal arm extends from the meeting point to the fly. A yellow isoceles triangle along the hoist nests in the opening of the pall.
history: The flag of the Republic of Tuva was adopted on 17 September 1992. The diverging white stripes symbolise the meeting of the Bii-Khem (Greater Yenisei) and Kaa-Khem (Lesser Yenisei) rivers, which join in Tuva to form the great Yenisei river.

features.symbolism: a white pall lying on its side, opening toward the hoist: two diagonal arms reach out toward the upper- and lower-hoist corners and a single horizontal arm extends to the fly; the white alludes to the confluence of the Bii-Khem (Greater Yenisei) and Kaa-Khem (Lesser Yenisei) rivers, which join in Tuva to form the Yenisei

features.role: yellow hoist triangle nested in the opening of the pall
features.symbolism: the sun and Buddhist tradition; wealth and prosperity of the Tuvan people
